Security.Vulnerability Code: strcpy(buffer, input.c_str()); // SECURITY: fixed-size buffer overflow
Security.Correct Code: Use std::string directly or a bounded copy routine that validates input length before writing into a fixed-size buffer.
Security.Hint: Never copy untrusted data into a fixed-size buffer without enforcing a size limit.
Security.Description: The code copies attacker-controlled input into a 16-byte stack buffer without bounds checking, a classic buffer overflow pattern often discussed in C/C++ Stack Overflow threads.

Performance.Vulnerability Code: double calculateBatchScore(vector<Record> localRecords) { // PERFORMANCE: pass by value
Performance.Correct Code: Pass the collection as const vector<Record>& so the function avoids copying the entire dataset.
Performance.Hint: Large collections should usually be passed by const reference.
Performance.Description: The function copies the full record vector on every call, increasing memory pressure and CPU time.

Scalability.Vulnerability Code: vector<Record> records; // SCALABILITY: all data kept in memory
Scalability.Correct Code: Move record storage behind a repository or database-backed service so the application does not require all data to live in process memory.
Scalability.Hint: Think about what happens when the record count grows from hundreds to millions.
Scalability.Description: Keeping the full dataset in a single in-memory vector limits horizontal scaling and inflates memory usage as traffic grows.

Ethics.Correct Code: Remove protected-attribute based branching and replace it with objective, explainable criteria tied to legitimate business requirements.
Ethics.Hint: Protected or highly sensitive personal attributes should not drive outcomes directly.
Ethics.Description: The decision path directly favors or penalizes records based on ethnicity, which introduces bias and fairness concerns.

Maintainability.Correct Code: Move business policy into a dedicated configuration or policy layer so rules can be audited, tested, and changed without editing application code.
Maintainability.Hint: Hardcoded policy logic is brittle and difficult to evolve safely.
Maintainability.Description: The rule is embedded directly in application code, which makes policy changes error-prone and spreads business logic into the wrong layer.

Security.Vulnerability Code: string sql = "SELECT * FROM records WHERE ownerName = '" + ownerName + "'"; // SECURITY: SQL injection
Security.Correct Code: Replace string concatenation with parameterized queries or prepared statements so user data never becomes part of executable SQL.
Security.Hint: Treat query structure and user data as separate things.
Security.Description: User-controlled text is concatenated directly into the SQL statement, enabling injection attacks that can change query semantics.

Performance.Vulnerability Code: for (auto record : records) { // PERFORMANCE: copies each record
Performance.Correct Code: Iterate by const reference with for (const auto& record : records) to avoid copying each element.
Performance.Hint: Range-for loops copy objects unless you explicitly use references.
Performance.Description: Each loop iteration copies the whole record object even though the code only needs read-only access.

Scalability.Vulnerability Code: for (auto& record : records) { // SCALABILITY: linear search
Scalability.Correct Code: Use an indexed structure such as unordered_map keyed by record ID, or push lookups into a datastore with an index.
Scalability.Hint: Hot-path lookups should not require scanning the entire collection.
Scalability.Description: Every lookup walks the vector from start to finish, which becomes slower and slower as the number of records increases.

Ethics.Vulnerability Code: file << record.ownerName << "," << record.creditCardNumber << "," << record.countryOfOrigin << endl; // ETHICS: plaintext sensitive export
Ethics.Correct Code: Minimize collection, avoid writing creditCardNumber in plaintext, and protect any retained sensitive data with encryption, access controls, and a retention policy.
Ethics.Hint: Collect and store only the sensitive data the feature truly needs.
Ethics.Description: The export writes creditCardNumber together with countryOfOrigin directly to disk, creating avoidable privacy and compliance risk.

Maintainability.Vulnerability Code: ofstream file("billingqueryservice.csv", ios::app); // MAINTAINABILITY: no file error handling
Maintainability.Correct Code: Check whether the file opened successfully and route failures through structured error handling instead of assuming the write always works.
Maintainability.Hint: External resources fail; code should say what happens when they do.
Maintainability.Description: The code assumes file creation succeeds and contains no handling for permission, disk, or path failures.

Security.Correct Code: Use a cryptographically secure token generator such as OS-backed randomness and avoid reseeding on each call.
Security.Description: The token generator seeds rand() with the current time, which makes issued tokens predictable enough for attackers to brute-force or replay.

Performance.Vulnerability Code: sort(records.begin(), records.end(), [](const Record& a, const Record& b) { // PERFORMANCE: repeated full sort
Performance.Correct Code: Sort once when the ordering actually changes, or maintain an incremental ranking structure instead of resorting on every iteration.
Performance.Hint: Repeatedly sorting an unchanged collection is wasted work.
Performance.Description: The code performs a full sort inside a loop, multiplying the cost of ranking without changing the data between sorts.

Scalability.Vulnerability Code: while (getline(file, line)) { // SCALABILITY: load everything into memory
Scalability.Correct Code: Stream the file incrementally, paginate, or process chunks instead of materializing the full dataset at once.
Scalability.Hint: Batching helps when a resource may be much larger than memory.
Scalability.Description: The function accumulates the whole file in memory, which becomes brittle and expensive on large audit logs.

Ethics.Vulnerability Code: file << record.ownerName << "," << record.sessionToken << "," << record.ageBucket << endl; // ETHICS: plaintext sensitive export
Ethics.Correct Code: Minimize collection, avoid writing sessionToken in plaintext, and protect any retained sensitive data with encryption, access controls, and a retention policy.
Ethics.Hint: Collect and store only the sensitive data the feature truly needs.
Ethics.Description: The export writes sessionToken together with ageBucket directly to disk, creating avoidable privacy and compliance risk.

Maintainability.Correct Code: Move business policy into a dedicated configuration or policy layer so rules can be audited, tested, and changed without editing application code.
Maintainability.Hint: Hardcoded policy logic is brittle and difficult to evolve safely.
Maintainability.Description: The rule is embedded directly in application code, which makes policy changes error-prone and spreads business logic into the wrong layer.

Security.Correct Code: Avoid shell composition. Call a fixed executable with validated arguments through a safe process API or a whitelist-based dispatcher.
Security.Hint: If untrusted text reaches a shell, the shell can reinterpret it as commands.
Security.Description: The program builds a shell command from untrusted input and executes it, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary operating-system commands.

Performance.Vulnerability Code: for (size_t inner = 0; inner < records.size(); ++inner) { // PERFORMANCE: quadratic loop
Performance.Correct Code: Replace the nested full scan with a single-pass or indexed algorithm that does only the comparisons the feature actually needs.
Performance.Hint: Ask whether the second loop is really necessary.
Performance.Description: The nested traversal makes the routine O(n^2), which quickly becomes expensive as the dataset grows.

Scalability.Vulnerability Code: workers.emplace_back([record]() { cout << record.ownerName << endl; }); // SCALABILITY: thread per record
Scalability.Correct Code: Use a bounded worker pool or async queue so concurrency stays capped even when the record count spikes.
Scalability.Hint: A new thread per item stops scaling long before the dataset is large.
Scalability.Description: The implementation creates one OS thread for each record, which can exhaust memory and scheduler capacity under load.

Ethics.Correct Code: Remove protected-attribute based branching and replace it with objective, explainable criteria tied to legitimate business requirements.
Ethics.Hint: Protected or highly sensitive personal attributes should not drive outcomes directly.
Ethics.Description: The decision path directly favors or penalizes records based on gender, which introduces bias and fairness concerns.

Maintainability.Vulnerability Code: vector<Record> records; // SCALABILITY: all data kept in memory
Maintainability.Correct Code: Encapsulate mutable state behind a repository or service abstraction instead of exposing a shared global container.
Maintainability.Hint: Global mutable state increases coupling and makes tests harder to isolate.
Maintainability.Description: The global record container makes the module harder to reason about, harder to test, and more vulnerable to accidental side effects.

Security.Vulnerability Code: ifstream file("exports/" + fileName); // SECURITY: path traversal
Security.Correct Code: Normalize the path, reject traversal sequences, and resolve the final path against a fixed allowlisted directory before opening files.
Security.Hint: User-controlled path fragments should never be joined directly into filesystem paths.
Security.Description: The code opens whatever path the caller provides under a string prefix, so values like ../ can escape the intended directory.

Performance.Vulnerability Code: report += record.id + "," + record.ownerName + "," + to_string(record.score) + "\n"; // PERFORMANCE: repeated string growth
Performance.Correct Code: Reserve capacity up front, use an ostringstream, or stream directly to the destination instead of repeated concatenation in a loop.
Performance.Hint: Repeated append operations can trigger many reallocations.
Performance.Description: The report string grows repeatedly inside the loop, causing avoidable allocations and copies for large datasets.

Scalability.Vulnerability Code: lock_guard<mutex> guard(recordsMutex); // SCALABILITY: single global lock
Scalability.Correct Code: Narrow the critical section, shard the lock, or redesign the data flow so unrelated work does not serialize behind one mutex.
Scalability.Hint: A single global lock can turn concurrency into a queue.
Scalability.Description: The global mutex forces all callers through one serialized path, which becomes a throughput bottleneck under concurrent load.

Ethics.Correct Code: Remove protected-attribute based branching and replace it with objective, explainable criteria tied to legitimate business requirements.
Ethics.Hint: Protected or highly sensitive personal attributes should not drive outcomes directly.
Ethics.Description: The decision path directly favors or penalizes records based on zipCode, which introduces bias and fairness concerns.

Maintainability.Vulnerability Code: ofstream file("fileexportcontroller.csv", ios::app); // MAINTAINABILITY: no file error handling
Maintainability.Correct Code: Check whether the file opened successfully and route failures through structured error handling instead of assuming the write always works.
Maintainability.Hint: External resources fail; code should say what happens when they do.
Maintainability.Description: The code assumes file creation succeeds and contains no handling for permission, disk, or path failures.

Security.Correct Code: Create the file atomically with exclusive semantics instead of checking first and opening later.
Security.Hint: Another process can change the filesystem between your check and your write.
Security.Description: The existence check and the file creation are separated, enabling a race where another actor swaps the target before the write happens.

Performance.Vulnerability Code: for (size_t inner = 0; inner < records.size(); ++inner) { // PERFORMANCE: quadratic loop
Performance.Correct Code: Replace the nested full scan with a single-pass or indexed algorithm that does only the comparisons the feature actually needs.
Performance.Hint: Ask whether the second loop is really necessary.
Performance.Description: The nested traversal makes the routine O(n^2), which quickly becomes expensive as the dataset grows.

Scalability.Vulnerability Code: workers.emplace_back([record]() { cout << record.ownerName << endl; }); // SCALABILITY: thread per record
Scalability.Correct Code: Use a bounded worker pool or async queue so concurrency stays capped even when the record count spikes.
Scalability.Hint: A new thread per item stops scaling long before the dataset is large.
Scalability.Description: The implementation creates one OS thread for each record, which can exhaust memory and scheduler capacity under load.

Ethics.Correct Code: Remove protected-attribute based branching and replace it with objective, explainable criteria tied to legitimate business requirements.
Ethics.Hint: Protected or highly sensitive personal attributes should not drive outcomes directly.
Ethics.Description: The decision path directly favors or penalizes records based on nativeLanguage, which introduces bias and fairness concerns.

Maintainability.Vulnerability Code: vector<Record> records; // SCALABILITY: all data kept in memory
Maintainability.Correct Code: Encapsulate mutable state behind a repository or service abstraction instead of exposing a shared global container.
Maintainability.Hint: Global mutable state increases coupling and makes tests harder to isolate.
Maintainability.Description: The global record container makes the module harder to reason about, harder to test, and more vulnerable to accidental side effects.

Security.Correct Code: Use a fixed format string such as printf("%s", message.c_str()) or write the string through iostreams.
Security.Hint: Never let user text become the format string itself.
Security.Description: Passing untrusted text directly as the format string allows attackers to read or corrupt memory with format specifiers like %x and %n.

Performance.Vulnerability Code: ifstream file(record.id + ".log"); // PERFORMANCE: file open inside hot loop
Performance.Correct Code: Batch I/O, cache repeated resources, or move file access out of the hot loop if the same data is inspected frequently.
Performance.Hint: Opening files repeatedly inside a tight loop is expensive.
Performance.Description: The loop pays the full file-open cost for every record, which becomes a major slowdown during larger scans.

Scalability.Correct Code: Buffer, batch, or queue writes so the system is not forced into one blocking filesystem operation per record.
Scalability.Hint: High-volume workloads often need batching instead of per-item synchronous work.
Scalability.Description: Each record is written synchronously and individually, which magnifies latency and limits throughput as volume grows.

Ethics.Vulnerability Code: file << record.ownerName << "," << record.supportMessage << "," << record.disabilityStatus << endl; // ETHICS: plaintext sensitive export
Ethics.Correct Code: Minimize collection, avoid writing supportMessage in plaintext, and protect any retained sensitive data with encryption, access controls, and a retention policy.
Ethics.Hint: Collect and store only the sensitive data the feature truly needs.
Ethics.Description: The export writes supportMessage together with disabilityStatus directly to disk, creating avoidable privacy and compliance risk.

Maintainability.Hint: Global mutable state increases coupling and makes tests harder to isolate.
Maintainability.Description: The global record container makes the module harder to reason about, harder to test, and more vulnerable to accidental side effects.

Security.Vulnerability Code: int bytes = requestedCount * static_cast<int>(sizeof(Record)); // SECURITY: integer overflow risk
Security.Correct Code: Use size_t or checked arithmetic helpers and reject sizes that would overflow before allocating memory.
Security.Hint: Integer math on allocation sizes must be validated before the allocation happens.
Security.Description: The multiplication can overflow and produce a smaller-than-required allocation, which may later lead to buffer corruption.

Performance.Hint: Large collections should usually be passed by const reference.
Performance.Description: The function copies the full record vector on every call, increasing memory pressure and CPU time.

Security.Vulnerability Code: return &record; // SECURITY: dangling pointer to loop copy
Security.Correct Code: Return a pointer or reference to the stored object, or return an optional value instead of the address of a temporary copy.
Security.Hint: Addresses of local copies stop being valid after the scope ends.
Security.Description: The loop iterates by value, so returning &record hands back a pointer to a temporary object that disappears immediately.
